title: Visualizing Nowhere Dense Sets and Baire's Category Theorem
description: A diagram illustrating the relationship between dense, nowhere dense, and meager sets, leading to the conclusion of Baire's Theorem.
elements: Dense Set D (e.g., Q in R)
Nowhere Dense Set N (e.g., a single point or Cantor set)
Meager Set M (countable union of nowhere dense sets)
Residual Set R (complement of a meager set)
relationships: N is a subset of X whose closure has empty interior
M is formed by a countable union of sets like N
Baire's Theorem: In a complete metric space, R is dense in X (and non-empty)
